title: caroxylon minutifolium
intro: Caroxylon minutifolium, also known as the "small-leaved paperbark," is a tree species native to the Pacific Island region. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits associated with Caroxylon minutifolium include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and support wound healing. Herbal preparations of Caroxylon minutifolium are typically made by infusing the bark in hot water to create a tea or decoction that can be used to treat various ailments.
